prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='DWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='FT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds show mixed trend directions (fold 1 down, folds 2-3 up) with significant seasonal amplitude of 163.66. High model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.73) and high seasonality variance (0.85) indicate models capture patterns very differently. Tier 1 models like ETS, THETA, ARIMA dominate overall RMSE while FT_svr leads in seasonality capture but has higher RMSE. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths without over-relying on any single pattern.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "DWT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "FT_svr",
  "overall_champion": "ETS",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "NaiveSeasonal", "late": "ETS"},
  "tier1_models": ["ETS", "THETA", "ARIMA", "FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "NaiveSeasonal", "rf", "CWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_DWT_rf", "catboost", "NaiveMovingAverage", "DWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "FT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.7294,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds show mixed trend directions (fold 1 down, folds 2-3 up) with significant seasonal amplitude of 163.66. High model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.73) and high seasonality variance (0.85) indicate models capture patterns very differently. Tier 1 models like ETS, THETA, ARIMA dominate overall RMSE while FT_svr leads in seasonality capture but has higher RMSE. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths without over-relying on any single pattern."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates high model heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread between ETS (137.49) and CWT_svr (274.54) is ~100%, justifying diverse candidate mix. pattern_analyst_insights recommends dba_combination for handling phase shifts and non-linear divergence with high seasonality variance (0.85). With n_windows=3, top_k=5 provides stability while inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 adds shrinkage to prevent overfitting. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific best performers."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage issues - all candidates use anti-leakage strategies (selection/weighted/baseline types never leak by design). Universe leaderboards show topk_mean_per_horizon_k2 has better RMSE (153.038) than current best (topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 at 155.577), but adding it would create redundancy with existing selection candidates. Current set is well-balanced with n=4, no pruning needed. All candidates are valid from universe.candidate_names.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds show mixed trend directions (fold 1 down, folds 2-3 up) with significant seasonal amplitude of 163.66. High model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.73) and high seasonality variance (0.85) indicate models capture patterns very differently. Tier 1 models like ETS, THETA, ARIMA dominate overall RMSE while FT_svr leads in seasonality capture but has higher RMSE. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths without over-relying on any single pattern.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates high model heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread between ETS (137.49) and CWT_svr (274.54) is ~100%, justifying diverse candidate mix. pattern_analyst_insights recommends dba_combination for handling phase shifts and non-linear divergence with high seasonality variance (0.85). With n_windows=3, top_k=5 provides stability while inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 adds shrinkage to prevent overfitting. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific best performers.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage issues - all candidates use anti-leakage strategies (selection/weighted/baseline types never leak by design). Universe leaderboards show topk_mean_per_horizon_k2 has better RMSE (153.038) than current best (topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 at 155.577), but adding it would create redundancy with existing selection candidates. Current set is well-balanced with n=4, no pruning needed. All candidates are valid from universe.candidate_names.
